mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, designed to achieve state-of-the-art inference performance with modern accelerators. It is ideal for production deployments requiring efficient orchestration of large language models, but may require careful resource management and configuration for optimal performance.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d command-line interface, which is necessary for managing deployments.

```bash

curl -Lo llm-d https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d && sudo mv llm-d /usr/local/bin/

```

> This command downloads the llm-d binary, makes it executable, and moves it to your PATH.
commands: curl -Lo llm-d https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d && sudo mv llm-d /usr/local/bin/

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in a chosen region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Replace 'us-central1' with your preferred region.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command fetches the cluster credentials and merges them into your kubeconfig.

steps.title: Verify Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in the cluster are ready and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up autoscaling and monitoring for the llm-d deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> This command applies the autoscaler configuration.
> This command sets up monitoring for the llm-d deployment.

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
This step removes the llm-d deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will lead to data loss and orphaned resources such as load balancers and PVCs.

```bash

kubectl delete deployment llm-d-deployment

gcloud container clusters delete llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> This command deletes the llm-d deployment.
> This command deletes the entire Kubernetes cluster.

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This step will upgrade llm-d to the latest version and verify the deployment.

```bash

kubectl get all -o yaml > llm-d-backup.yaml

kubectl set image deployment/llm-d-deployment llm-d=ghcr.io/llm-d/llm-d:v0.5.1

kubectl rollout status deployment/llm-d-deployment

```

> This command backs up the current state of all resources.
> This command updates the deployment to the latest version.
> This command checks the status of the rollout to ensure it was successful.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image cannot be found or accessed.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and tag in your deployment configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application is crashing on startup due to misconfiguration or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Pending' status for pods
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient resources available in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Scale up the cluster or adjust resource requests/limits in your deployment.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'FailedScheduling'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** No nodes are available that match the pod's resource requests.

**Fix:**
Check node resource availability and adjust pod resource requests accordingly.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new hardware accelerators and improved inference scheduling algorithms.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods' showing all pods in 'Running' status, indicating that llm-d is successfully deployed.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
